title: Isaac Bell House, Second Floor Plan (HABS RI-308, sheet 3)
era_or_year: house built 1881-1883 (McKim, Mead & White); HABS drawings 1969
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey; design architects McKim, Mead & White; delineator Thomas B. Schubert
research_notes: Plan is sheet 3 (00003v.jpg) of the HABS RI-308 'Drawings' resource (resource index 1; resource 0 is photographs). Title block reads 'Second Floor Plan, Sheet 3 of 8'.
research_features: Large round corner tower (true curved walls), multiple staircases, deep wraparound porch with curved steps, irregular Shingle Style massing. Excellent curved-wall + complex-circulation test case.
feature_highlights: large round corner tower with genuinely curved exterior walls enclosing a round room
second curved, apsidal bay projecting from the lower plan
fan of curved porch steps splaying outward in an arc
multiple staircases including a dogleg and a curved stair
sprawling, irregular stepped-and-jogged Shingle Style footprint
dimensioned overall runs and a graphic scale bar
supported_examples: straight walls at right angles for the rectilinear remainder of the plan
rooms derived from walls; room naming and labels
linear point-to-point dimensions in imperial units
roadmap_examples: multi-floor (second floor of a stacked house)

gap_features.why: the round corner tower and the apsidal bay have truly curved exterior walls and circular/apsidal room polygons that straight-segment Phase-1 walls cannot represent
the porch reached by the curved steps is a roofed but open space, not a fully wall-enclosed room
